itemSummaries.title: How BM25 and RAG Retrieve Information Differently?
itemSummaries.summary: 本文对比了传统BM25检索算法与新型RAG检索方式在信息查找上的核心差异，BM25基于词频和文档长度等三要素评分，而RAG结合大语言模型实现更智能的语义检索。
itemSummaries.recommendation: 建议近期纳入评估《How BM25 and RAG Retrieve Information Differently?》相关方案，重点关注实践步骤的可复现性。

itemSummaries.recommendation: 适合计算材料科学领域开发者学习pymatgen基础操作，特别是需要材料项目集成和相图分析的工程团队。

itemSummaries.title: Safely Deploying ML Models to Production: Four Controlled Strategies (A/B, Canary, Interleaved, Shadow Testing)
itemSummaries.titleZh: 安全部署ML模型到生产环境：四种受控策略（A/B、金丝雀、交错、影子测试）

itemSummaries.recommendation: 工程团队在部署新ML模型时应采用受控策略，避免直接替换导致线上事故，建议从影子测试开始逐步验证。

itemSummaries.title: AWS Expands Aurora DSQL with Playground, New Tool Integrations, and Driver Connectors

itemSummaries.recommendation: 该案例展示了AI在安全测试中的强大能力，工程团队应关注AI辅助漏洞发现的趋势及其对开发流程的影响。

leadSummary: 本期重点围绕research与tooling展开，建议优先关注《使用 RLax JAX Haiku Optax 从零实现深度 Q 学习 DQN》、《认识 GitAgent：解决 LangChain、AutoGen、Claude Code 碎片化的 AI Agent 容器》、《Stripe 工程师部署 Minions：每周生成上千条 PR 的自主代理》，并结合现有工程路线评估接入优先级与落地成本。

categoryLeadSummaries.lead: 工具链方向本期共 4 条，建议优先阅读《认识 GitAgent：解决 LangChain、AutoGen、Claude Code 碎片化的 AI Agent 容器》、《QCon London 2026：摩根士丹利为 MCP 时代重新设计 API 计划》，重点关注其工程可落地性与集成成本。
研究方向本期共 4 条，建议优先阅读《使用 RLax JAX Haiku Optax 从零实现深度 Q 学习 DQN》、《浙大团队破解多模态模型「盲目自信」：先校准置信度，再分配算力丨CVPR’26》，重点关注其工程可落地性与集成成本。
行业动态本期共 4 条，建议优先阅读《Cursor admits its new coding model was built on top of Moonshot AI's Kimi》、《上海开发者大会3月开幕锁定WAIC直通卡》，重点关注其工程可落地性与集成成本。
教程实践本期共 3 条，建议优先阅读《构建不确定性感知的LLM系统实战教程》、《BM25 与 RAG 如何不同地检索信息？》，重点关注其工程可落地性与集成成本。

highlights.title: Implementing Deep Q-Learning (DQN) from Scratch Using RLax JAX Haiku and Optax to Train a CartPole Reinforcement Learning Agent
highlights.contentSnippet: In this tutorial, we implement a reinforcement learning agent using RLax, a research-oriented library developed by Google DeepMind for building reinforcement learning algorithms with JAX. We combine RLax with JAX, Haiku, and Optax to construct a Deep Q-Learning (DQN) agent that learns to solve the CartPole environment. Instead of using a fully packaged RL framework, […] The post Implementing Deep Q-Learning (DQN) from Scratch Using RLax JAX Haiku and Optax to Train a CartPole Reinforcement Learning Agent appeared first on MarkTechPost.
highlights.titleZh: 使用 RLax JAX Haiku Optax 从零实现深度 Q 学习 DQN
highlights.llmClassifyReason: 深度强化学习 DQN 实现，基于 JAX/RLax 的算法研究。

highlights.title: Meet GitAgent: The Docker for AI Agents that is Finally Solving the Fragmentation between LangChain, AutoGen, and Claude Code
highlights.contentSnippet: The current state of AI agent development is characterized by significant architectural fragmentation. Software devs building autonomous systems must generally commit to one of several competing ecosystems: LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, OpenAI Assistants, or the more recent Claude Code. Each of these ‘Five Frameworks’ utilizes a proprietary method for defining agent logic, memory persistence, and tool […] The post Meet GitAgent: The Docker for AI Agents that is Finally Solving the Fragmentation between LangChain, AutoGen, and Claude Code appeared first on MarkTechPost.
highlights.titleZh: 认识 GitAgent：解决 LangChain、AutoGen、Claude Code 碎片化的 AI Agent 容器
highlights.llmClassifyReason: 解决 Agent 生态碎片化的工具，聚焦工程实践。

highlights.title: Stripe Engineers Deploy Minions, Autonomous Agents Producing Thousands of Pull Requests Weekly
highlights.contentSnippet: Stripe engineers describe Minions, autonomous coding agents generating over 1,300 pull requests per week. Tasks can originate from Slack, bug reports, or feature requests. Using LLMs, blueprints, and CI/CD pipelines, Minions produce production-ready changes while maintaining reliability and human review. By Leela Kumili
highlights.titleZh: Stripe 工程师部署 Minions：每周生成上千条 PR 的自主代理
highlights.llmClassifyReason: Stripe 生产级自主代理实践，每周千级 PR，工程价值极高。

highlights.title: Where Do Humans Fit in AI-Assisted Software Development?
highlights.contentSnippet: An article on Martin Fowler’s blog by Kief Morris examines the role of humans in AI-assisted software engineering, arguing developers are unlikely to move fully “out of the loop.” Instead, teams may work “on the loop,” designing tests, specifications, and feedback mechanisms to guide AI agents, as industry discussions focus on how such systems should be verified and governed. By Matt Foster
highlights.titleZh: 人类在 AI 辅助软件开发中扮演什么角色？
highlights.llmClassifyReason: 探讨人类在 AI 辅助开发中的角色，Agent 实践反思。

highlights.title: A Coding Implementation to Build an Uncertainty-Aware LLM System with Confidence Estimation, Self-Evaluation, and Automatic Web Research
highlights.contentSnippet: In this tutorial, we build an uncertainty-aware large language model system that not only generates answers but also estimates the confidence in those answers. We implement a three-stage reasoning pipeline in which the model first produces an answer along with a self-reported confidence score and a justification. We then introduce a self-evaluation step that allows […] The post A Coding Implementation to Build an Uncertainty-Aware LLM System with Confidence Estimation, Self-Evaluation, and Automatic Web Research appeared first on MarkTechPost.

rankedItems.title: Show HN: AI models AI capex
rankedItems.contentSnippet: Bernie interviewed Claude recently (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h3AtWdeu_G0). The one-shotted responses were predictably a little sycophantic, but some responses were a little surprising. Made me think I'm kinda doing something similar in a more permissive timeframe, I have a harness over Claude that "persistently" studies AI spending, it does deep research, responds to my inputs, persists notes, writes code, etc. It's not really "introspection" or "Cogito, ergo sum", but I found that over tens of millions of tokens of iterations the system was able to create something novel. I am showing off one of the dashboards it created based on the deep research and Python modelling conducted: https://ai-capex-sens-wxc4b.ondigitalocean.app/ --- TLDR; AI hype is one thing, but how else would you actually go pedal to the metal spending the GDP of a middle income country on new AI data centers? Basically, theres hundreds of billions of dollars in cloud business from enterprise contracts, boring IT stuff but its where a lot of the money in tech comes from and its real. Beyond the pontification about superintelligence is a similar reality for AI in the eyes of the hyperscalers that scaled the cloud business in the past, hundreds of billions to trillions in business from enterprise contracts (cloud replaced on-prem servers, AI replaces or augments knowledge workers). The model estimates AI revenue from automatable knowledge-work spend, stress it with adoption/pricing assumptions, and run it against capex + operating + financing costs under delays/shocks. In the base case, it doesn’t break even by 2032, but of course there's other sources of revenue so this isn't all that doomer or surprising that its risky to spend so much. However, its nice to have numbers and sensitivity to back that conclusion up instead of hand waving it. --- There's been a lot of AI mania over vibe coding, but the main conclusion I got from running this harness is we can in fact use AI harnesses to simply think and make better decisions too. In this case, the AI is reducing the burden of injecting rigor into writing and decision making. Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47484145 Points: 1 # Comments: 0
rankedItems.llmClassifyReason: 关于AI模型CAPEX的讨论，偏向成本研究分析。

rankedItems.title: Ask HN: How much are you spending on AI coding at work?
rankedItems.contentSnippet: Jensen Huang recently said that he thinks an engineer who makes $500k should spend at least $250k a year on “tokens” which is an astounding figure. I personally don’t know how I could spend that much if I tried. Obviously he has a huge financial incentive to convince people that $250k per engineer is reasonable, but it got me thinking that it’s time for a survey. How much are you and your coworkers spending on AI coding tools at work? I’m talking about Cursor, Claude Code, etc. Not all AI-powered SaaS, just the stuff that’s metered by token. Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47484203 Points: 3 # Comments: 1
rankedItems.llmClassifyReason: HN社区关于AI编码成本的讨论，涉及工具定价。

itemSummaries.title: What would you do if you have AI software that may be transformers alternative?
itemSummaries.titleZh: 如果你有一个可能替代transformers的AI软件，你会怎么做？
itemSummaries.summary: 开发者询问其开发的AI软件能否成为Transformer架构的替代品，该软件自称在速度和智能方面有10倍提升，采用C/C#/F#实现，代码量约10-15k行。
itemSummaries.recommendation: 关注非Transformer架构的演进趋势，适合对新型高效模型架构感兴趣的工程团队了解行业前沿探索。

quickDigest.title: 认识 GitAgent：解决 LangChain、AutoGen、Claude Code 碎片化的 AI Agent 容器 (Meet GitAgent: The Docker for AI Agents that is Finally Solving the Fragmentation between LangChain, AutoGen, and Claude Code)
quickDigest.takeaway: GitAgent 是一个新兴的 AI Agent 工具，旨在统一当前碎片化的 AI Agent 框架生态，解决 LangChain、AutoGen、CrewAI 等五大框架之间的互操作性问题。

quickDigest.takeaway: Morgan Stanley engineers Jim Gough and Andreea Niculcea showed how they're retooling the bank's API program for AI agen…

quickDigest.title: BM25 与 RAG 如何不同地检索信息？ (How BM25 and RAG Retrieve Information Differently?)
quickDigest.takeaway: 本文对比了传统BM25检索算法与新型RAG检索方式在信息查找上的核心差异，BM25基于词频和文档长度等三要素评分，而RAG结合大语言模型实现更智能的语义检索。

quickDigest.title: 使用 Pymatgen 构建和分析晶体结构：对称性分析、相图、表面生成 (A Coding Implementation for Building and Analyzing Crystal Structures Using Pymatgen for Symmetry Analysis, Phase Diagrams, Surface Generation, and Materials Project Integration)
quickDigest.takeaway: 本教程介绍使用Python的pymatgen库进行晶体结构构建与分析，涵盖硅、氯化钠、LiFePO₄等材料的晶格性质、对称性分析、原子配位环境和氧化态应用。

quickDigest.title: 安全部署ML模型到生产环境：四种受控策略（A/B、金丝雀、交错、影子测试） (Safely Deploying ML Models to Production: Four Controlled Strategies (A/B, Canary, Interleaved, Shadow Testing))
quickDigest.takeaway: 本文介绍了四种安全的ML模型部署策略：A/B测试、金丝雀发布、交错测试和影子测试，帮助在生产环境中逐步验证模型效果并降低风险。

quickDigest.title: AWS 扩展 Aurora DSQL：新增 Playground、工具集成和驱动连接器 (AWS Expands Aurora DSQL with Playground, New Tool Integrations, and Driver Connectors)
quickDigest.takeaway: AWS为Aurora DSQL推出多项更新，包括新的交互式Playground（可直接在浏览器中免费体验）、新工具集成和驱动连接器，旨在提升可用性和开发者体验。

snapshot.leadSummary: 本期重点围绕research与tooling展开，建议优先关注《使用 RLax JAX Haiku Optax 从零实现深度 Q 学习 DQN》、《认识 GitAgent：解决 LangChain、AutoGen、Claude Code 碎片化的 AI Agent 容器》、《Stripe 工程师部署 Minions：每周生成上千条 PR 的自主代理》，并结合现有工程路线评估接入优先级与落地成本。

categoryLeadSummaries.lead: 工具链方向本期共 4 条，建议优先阅读《认识 GitAgent：解决 LangChain、AutoGen、Claude Code 碎片化的 AI Agent 容器》、《QCon London 2026：摩根士丹利为 MCP 时代重新设计 API 计划》，重点关注其工程可落地性与集成成本。
研究方向本期共 4 条，建议优先阅读《使用 RLax JAX Haiku Optax 从零实现深度 Q 学习 DQN》、《浙大团队破解多模态模型「盲目自信」：先校准置信度，再分配算力丨CVPR’26》，重点关注其工程可落地性与集成成本。
行业动态本期共 4 条，建议优先阅读《Cursor admits its new coding model was built on top of Moonshot AI's Kimi》、《上海开发者大会3月开幕锁定WAIC直通卡》，重点关注其工程可落地性与集成成本。
教程实践本期共 3 条，建议优先阅读《构建不确定性感知的LLM系统实战教程》、《BM25 与 RAG 如何不同地检索信息？》，重点关注其工程可落地性与集成成本。

highlights.title: Implementing Deep Q-Learning (DQN) from Scratch Using RLax JAX Haiku and Optax to Train a CartPole Reinforcement Learning Agent
highlights.contentSnippet: In this tutorial, we implement a reinforcement learning agent using RLax, a research-oriented library developed by Google DeepMind for building reinforcement learning algorithms with JAX. We combine RLax with JAX, Haiku, and Optax to construct a Deep Q-Learning (DQN) agent that learns to solve the CartPole environment. Instead of using a fully packaged RL framework, […] The post Implementing Deep Q-Learning (DQN) from Scratch Using RLax JAX Haiku and Optax to Train a CartPole Reinforcement Learning Agent appeared first on MarkTechPost.
highlights.titleZh: 使用 RLax JAX Haiku Optax 从零实现深度 Q 学习 DQN
highlights.llmClassifyReason: 深度强化学习 DQN 实现，基于 JAX/RLax 的算法研究。

highlights.title: Meet GitAgent: The Docker for AI Agents that is Finally Solving the Fragmentation between LangChain, AutoGen, and Claude Code
highlights.contentSnippet: The current state of AI agent development is characterized by significant architectural fragmentation. Software devs building autonomous systems must generally commit to one of several competing ecosystems: LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, OpenAI Assistants, or the more recent Claude Code. Each of these ‘Five Frameworks’ utilizes a proprietary method for defining agent logic, memory persistence, and tool […] The post Meet GitAgent: The Docker for AI Agents that is Finally Solving the Fragmentation between LangChain, AutoGen, and Claude Code appeared first on MarkTechPost.
highlights.titleZh: 认识 GitAgent：解决 LangChain、AutoGen、Claude Code 碎片化的 AI Agent 容器
highlights.llmClassifyReason: 解决 Agent 生态碎片化的工具，聚焦工程实践。

highlights.title: Stripe Engineers Deploy Minions, Autonomous Agents Producing Thousands of Pull Requests Weekly
highlights.contentSnippet: Stripe engineers describe Minions, autonomous coding agents generating over 1,300 pull requests per week. Tasks can originate from Slack, bug reports, or feature requests. Using LLMs, blueprints, and CI/CD pipelines, Minions produce production-ready changes while maintaining reliability and human review. By Leela Kumili
highlights.titleZh: Stripe 工程师部署 Minions：每周生成上千条 PR 的自主代理
highlights.llmClassifyReason: Stripe 生产级自主代理实践，每周千级 PR，工程价值极高。

highlights.title: Where Do Humans Fit in AI-Assisted Software Development?
highlights.contentSnippet: An article on Martin Fowler’s blog by Kief Morris examines the role of humans in AI-assisted software engineering, arguing developers are unlikely to move fully “out of the loop.” Instead, teams may work “on the loop,” designing tests, specifications, and feedback mechanisms to guide AI agents, as industry discussions focus on how such systems should be verified and governed. By Matt Foster
highlights.titleZh: 人类在 AI 辅助软件开发中扮演什么角色？
highlights.llmClassifyReason: 探讨人类在 AI 辅助开发中的角色，Agent 实践反思。

highlights.title: A Coding Implementation to Build an Uncertainty-Aware LLM System with Confidence Estimation, Self-Evaluation, and Automatic Web Research
highlights.contentSnippet: In this tutorial, we build an uncertainty-aware large language model system that not only generates answers but also estimates the confidence in those answers. We implement a three-stage reasoning pipeline in which the model first produces an answer along with a self-reported confidence score and a justification. We then introduce a self-evaluation step that allows […] The post A Coding Implementation to Build an Uncertainty-Aware LLM System with Confidence Estimation, Self-Evaluation, and Automatic Web Research appeared first on MarkTechPost.

highlights.llmClassifyReason: 构建不确定性感知LLM系统的完整教程，含代码实现。
